message:
fix(codegen): Nested classes with captures use .init() not .call()

Nested classes defined inside functions were incorrectly treated as
closures when they had captured variables. This caused `C.call()` to
be generated instead of `C.init()`, resulting in "use of undeclared
identifier 'C'" errors since the struct has no call() method.

Fixed by excluding nested_class_names from the closure_vars check in
the call expression generator. Nested classes should always use .init()
for instantiation, even if they have captured variables.
